methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $2.5M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises five private companies operating in the same sector—Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—though no public comparables exist in this space.

Given the early stage and absence of sufficient narrow-set peers at the pre-seed or adjacent stages, only one valuation method proved applicable. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $2.5M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $6M. This method rests on the assumption that capital deployed approximates early-stage value and that step-up multiples hold across the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. The narrow method count—one applicable approach—represents the primary limitation. A more robust valuation would benefit from either access to recent funding rounds or transaction data from the five-company peer set, or from establishing sufficient comparability on stage, growth trajectory, or technology maturity. Until additional data surfaces, reliance on stage-based progression remains the most defensible quantitative anchor, though the range should be treated as indicative rather than conclusive.
